title: Decode a Keycloak Access Token
description:
## Inspect a JWT

Keycloak issues access tokens as **JWTs**: three base64url parts (`header.payload.signature`) joined by dots. The middle part is a base64url-encoded JSON payload of claims.

A token has been placed at **`/root/token.jwt`**.

**Your task:** decode the **payload** and write the value of the **`preferred_username`** claim to **`/root/username.txt`**.

> 💡 Take the 2nd dot-separated field and base64url-decode it. The hint shows a one-liner; you must add back base64 padding (URL-safe base64 strips it).

hints.title: Decode the payload claim
hints.body: Split on dots, base64url-decode the payload (re-add padding), parse the JSON, and print the claim.

hints.title: Pure shell alternative
hints.body: You can also do it with cut, tr (url-safe to standard), base64 -d and jq.

validation.description: Checks that /root/username.txt holds the preferred_username claim (alice).
